exact_sentence: We help teams take repetitive internal processes out of email and spreadsheets and turn them into practical automations people actually use.
That can cover things like approvals, notifications, data movement, exception handling and simple internal workflows without a heavyweight rebuild.
If useful, I can sketch where automation would remove the most admin first.
We build Power BI dashboards that replace spreadsheet-heavy reporting with something leadership can actually use day to day.
That usually means clearer reporting across sales, stock, finance or operations without asking the team to maintain another manual process.
Happy to show the kind of reporting structure we would put in place.
We help teams clean up and rebuild CRM setups so the pipeline, reporting and day-to-day usage stop fighting each other.
That can mean restructuring properties and pipelines, fixing data issues, tightening reporting and making the system easier for the team to use properly.
If helpful, I can outline the sort of CRM cleanup work that tends to make the biggest difference first.
We help retail teams make better use of sales, stock and trading data so reporting is quicker and decisions are based on the same numbers.
That often means bringing fragmented sales and stock reporting into one view that is easier to trust and act on.
I can share an example of how we would structure that view for a retail team.
We help teams pull marketing data into cleaner reporting so channel performance is easier to understand without manual weekly wrangling.
That can bring campaign, spend and conversion reporting into one place so the team can spend more time acting on it than assembling it.
If useful, I can show the kind of reporting layer we normally build around marketing data.
We help teams analyse comments and free-text feedback so recurring issues and themes are easier to spot at scale.
That can sit alongside operational or commercial reporting so customer feedback becomes more useful than a long list of comments.
Happy to share how we would approach comment analysis without overcomplicating it.
A simple starting point is a short diagnostic of where reporting, CRM or internal processes are creating avoidable manual work.
We usually use that to show where the quickest wins are before anyone commits to a larger piece of work.
If that is easier, we could start there and keep it practical.
